title: Quincy gas station looking for violist to ‘serenade’ customers
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Harvey Kertzman, owner of Quincy Gas, is seeking a violist to serenade customers during warm weather.
Signs at 451 Quincy Ave advertise the need for a violist; guitarists have applied but are not sought.
A retired violist has agreed to perform; the search continues for additional musicians as backup.

title: Report: Patriots agree to sign edge rusher Dre’Mont Jones
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots signing Dre'Mont Jones for three years, up to $39.5 million, reports NFL Media.
Jones had a career-high seven sacks and 24 QB hits last season with Titans and Ravens.
Patriots finished 26th in sacks and pressures in 2025; Jones brings experience and versatility.

title: How a Boston Marathon runner finds purpose in mentoring and miles
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Victoria Walker, 31, from Brighton, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Minds Matter Boston.
Walker mentors high-achieving, low-income students, helping them with college applications and building confidence.
Running provides Walker with structure and community, connecting her with Boston and motivating her marathon efforts.

title: Meet the New England Aquarium’s viral seal and his rubber duckie pal
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Reggae, a harbor seal at the New England Aquarium, went viral for interacting with a rubber duck toy.
The video featuring Reggae has garnered nearly 500,000 views, highlighting seal intelligence and trainer efforts.
Aquarium trainers engage seals in cognitive enrichment activities to simulate natural behaviors and enhance wellbeing.

title: What we know about Bravo’s ‘The Real Housewives of Rhode Island’
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premieres April 2 on Bravo, celebrating 20 years of the franchise.
Fenway Park hosts a Bravo Night on April 17 with the RHORI cast attending the Red Sox game against the Tigers.
The cast includes a former news anchor, pizzeria co-owner, and Rhode Island's 'Cannabis Queen.'

title: 24 Boston businesses approved for new or upgraded liquor licenses
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Boston approved 24 new or upgraded liquor licenses for local restaurants, enhancing their ability to serve alcoholic beverages.
The licenses, part of a 2024 legislation, are mainly zip code-restricted to support neighborhoods like East Boston and Roxbury.
All approved licenses are non-transferable, reverting to the city once not in use, encouraging equitable distribution.

title: Mass. man nabbed after allegedly driving over 100 mph in N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 21-year-old Attleboro man was arrested for driving 104 mph in a 55 mph zone on Route 202, Rindge.
The vehicle, a 2020 Kia Stinger, was stopped by Rindge Police near Sears Drive after a speed report.
The man faces charges of reckless operation and potential penalties including a fine and 90-day license suspension.

title: AirTag leads volunteers to hidden cat and six kittens in Waltham
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Volunteers from The Cat Connection used an AirTag to locate a stray cat and six kittens in Waltham.
Rescuers found the cat family hidden in debris at a self-storage facility and moved them to a foster home.
Emerald and her kittens will be available for adoption in about two months, announced the nonprofit organization.

title: N.H. Senate approves toll hike for out-of-state drivers
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: N.H. Senate passed a bill to increase tolls for out-of-state drivers, aiming to fund transportation infrastructure.
Governor Kelly Ayotte opposes the toll hike bill, which now moves to the N.H. House of Representatives.
Projected revenue from toll increases could rise from $53.3 million in 2027 to $81.4 million by 2036.

title: One dead after crash in Tewksbury
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: One person died in a head-on collision Sunday on Andover Street near Cooney Road in Tewksbury, police report.
Both drivers were treated on-site before being transported to local hospitals; one driver succumbed to their injuries.
Andover Street was closed for several hours Sunday night for the crash reconstruction by Tewksbury Police.
